kids.question: Who wrote the letter to the Colossians?
kids.explanation: Colossians 1:1 — "Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God."

kids.question: What did Paul say the Colossians had in Christ Jesus?
kids.explanation: Colossians 1:4 — "Since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus."

kids.question: What did Paul pray the Colossians would be filled with?
kids.explanation: Colossians 1:9 — "That ye might be filled with the knowledge of his will."

teens.question: According to Colossians 1:13, from what has God delivered believers?
teens.option_a: The power of darkness
teens.explanation: Colossians 1:13 — "Who hath delivered us from the power of darkness."

teens.question: What does Colossians 1:15 say about Jesus in relation to the invisible God?
teens.option_a: He is the image of the invisible God
teens.option_b: He is a prophet of God
teens.option_c: He is an angel of God
teens.option_d: He is a servant of God
teens.explanation: Colossians 1:15 — "Who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of every creature."

teens.question: According to verse 16, what was created by Jesus?
teens.option_a: All things in heaven and earth
teens.explanation: Colossians 1:16 — "For by him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth."

teens.question: What does Paul say holds all things together?
teens.option_a: Christ
teens.explanation: Colossians 1:17 — "And he is before all things, and by him all things consist."

teens.question: What is Christ called in relation to the church in verse 18?
teens.option_a: The head of the body
teens.explanation: Colossians 1:18 — "And he is the head of the body, the church."

adults.question: In Colossians 1:14, what do believers have through Christ's blood?
adults.option_a: Redemption through his blood, even the forgiveness of sins
adults.explanation: Colossians 1:14 — "In whom we have redemption through his blood, even the forgiveness of sins."

adults.question: What phrase does Paul use in verse 15 to describe Christ's relation to creation?
adults.option_a: The firstborn of every creature
adults.explanation: Colossians 1:15 — "The firstborn of every creature." This denotes supremacy and preeminence, not that Christ was created.

adults.question: According to verse 19, what was the Father's pleasure regarding Christ?
adults.option_a: That in him should all fulness dwell
adults.option_b: That he should serve as a prophet only
adults.option_c: That he should reign on earth immediately
adults.option_d: That he should remain hidden from mankind
adults.explanation: Colossians 1:19 — "For it pleased the Father that in him should all fulness dwell."

adults.question: How does verse 20 say peace was made between God and creation?
adults.option_a: Through the blood of his cross
adults.option_b: Through the keeping of the law
adults.explanation: Colossians 1:20 — "Having made peace through the blood of his cross."

adults.question: What does Paul say the gospel was preached to in verse 23?
adults.option_a: Every creature which is under heaven
adults.explanation: Colossians 1:23 — "Which was preached to every creature which is under heaven."

adults.question: What does Paul say he fills up in verse 24?
adults.option_a: That which is behind of the afflictions of Christ in his flesh
adults.explanation: Colossians 1:24 — "Fill up that which is behind of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh."

adults.question: What is the mystery hidden from ages and generations according to verse 26-27?
adults.option_a: Christ in you, the hope of glory
adults.option_d: The location of the Garden of Eden
adults.explanation: Colossians 1:27 — "Which is Christ in you, the hope of glory."

adults.question: What is Paul's stated goal in preaching Christ according to verse 28?
adults.option_a: To present every man perfect in Christ Jesus
adults.option_b: To build the largest church possible
adults.explanation: Colossians 1:28 — "That we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus."

kids.question: Where does Paul say all treasures of wisdom and knowledge are hidden?
kids.explanation: Colossians 2:3 — "In whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge."

kids.question: What did Paul warn the Colossians not to be tricked by?
kids.explanation: Colossians 2:4 — "Lest any man should beguile you with enticing words."

kids.question: How does Paul say believers should walk?
kids.option_a: As they received Christ, walk in him
kids.explanation: Colossians 2:6 — "As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk ye in him."

teens.question: What does Paul warn the Colossians to beware of in verse 8?
teens.option_a: Philosophy and vain deceit
teens.explanation: Colossians 2:8 — "Beware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit."

teens.question: What dwells in Christ bodily according to verse 9?
teens.option_a: All the fulness of the Godhead
teens.explanation: Colossians 2:9 — "For in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily."

teens.question: What did Christ do with the handwriting of ordinances against us?
teens.option_a: Nailed it to his cross
teens.explanation: Colossians 2:14 — "Blotting out the handwriting of ordinances that was against us... nailing it to his cross."

teens.question: What should no man judge believers about according to verse 16?
teens.option_a: Meat, drink, holy days, new moon, or sabbath days
teens.explanation: Colossians 2:16 — "Let no man therefore judge you in meat, or in drink, or in respect of an holyday."

teens.question: What does Paul call these Jewish observances in verse 17?
teens.option_a: A shadow of things to come
teens.explanation: Colossians 2:17 — "Which are a shadow of things to come; but the body is of Christ."

adults.question: What does Paul describe as the "circumcision of Christ" in verse 11?
adults.option_a: Putting off the body of the sins of the flesh
adults.explanation: Colossians 2:11 — "In whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, in putting off the body of the sins of the flesh."

adults.question: According to verse 12, with what are believers buried with Christ?
adults.option_a: Baptism
adults.explanation: Colossians 2:12 — "Buried with him in baptism, wherein also ye are risen with him."

adults.question: What did God do to principalities and powers according to verse 15?
adults.option_a: Spoiled them and made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them
adults.option_b: Gave them more power and authority
adults.explanation: Colossians 2:15 — "Having spoiled principalities and powers, he made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them in it."

adults.question: What does Paul say about voluntary humility and worshipping of angels in verse 18?
adults.option_a: Let no man beguile you of your reward through such things
adults.option_b: These are acceptable forms of worship
adults.option_d: They are the highest form of devotion
adults.explanation: Colossians 2:18 — "Let no man beguile you of your reward in a voluntary humility and worshipping of angels."

adults.question: According to verse 19, what happens when believers do not hold the Head (Christ)?
adults.option_a: The body cannot grow with the increase of God
adults.explanation: Colossians 2:19 — "Not holding the Head, from which all the body... increaseth with the increase of God."

adults.question: If believers are dead with Christ from the rudiments of the world, what should they not do (v. 20)?
adults.option_a: Be subject to ordinances as though living in the world
adults.explanation: Colossians 2:20 — "If ye be dead with Christ from the rudiments of the world, why... are ye subject to ordinances."

adults.question: What are the "touch not; taste not; handle not" rules described as in verse 22?
adults.option_a: Commandments and doctrines of men that perish with the using
adults.explanation: Colossians 2:22 — "Which all are to perish with the using; after the commandments and doctrines of men."

adults.question: Despite having a show of wisdom, what do man-made rules fail to do according to verse 23?
adults.option_a: Honour the body or restrain the indulgence of the flesh
adults.explanation: Colossians 2:23 — "Which things have indeed a shew of wisdom... not in any honour to the satisfying of the flesh."

kids.question: What does Paul say believers should seek?
kids.option_a: Things which are above, where Christ sits
kids.explanation: Colossians 3:1 — "Seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God."

kids.question: What does Paul tell the Colossians to put on?
kids.explanation: Colossians 3:12,14 — "Put on therefore... kindness... And above all these things put on charity."

kids.question: What should children do according to this chapter?
kids.option_a: Obey their parents in all things
kids.explanation: Colossians 3:20 — "Children, obey your parents in all things: for this is well pleasing unto the Lord."

teens.question: What does Paul say believers should set their affection on?
teens.option_a: Things above, not on things on the earth
teens.option_b: Things of the earth for daily living
teens.explanation: Colossians 3:2 — "Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth."

teens.question: What five sins does Paul list first to "mortify" (put to death) in verse 5?
teens.option_a: Fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness
teens.option_b: Lying, stealing, murder, slander, and gossip
teens.option_c: Pride, envy, gluttony, sloth, and greed
teens.option_d: Anger, bitterness, malice, wrath, and revenge
teens.explanation: Colossians 3:5 — "Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness."

teens.question: According to verse 11, in the new man there is neither what?
teens.option_a: Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond nor free
teens.explanation: Colossians 3:11 — "Where there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond nor free: but Christ is all, and in all."

teens.question: What is called "the bond of perfectness" in verse 14?
teens.option_a: Charity (love)
teens.explanation: Colossians 3:14 — "And above all these things put on charity, which is the bond of perfectness."

teens.question: How does Paul say believers should do everything according to verse 17?
teens.option_a: In the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God
teens.option_b: In their own strength and wisdom
teens.explanation: Colossians 3:17 — "Whatsoever ye do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God."

adults.question: What does Paul say about believers' life being hid in verse 3?
adults.option_a: Your life is hid with Christ in God
adults.option_b: Your life is displayed for all to see
adults.option_c: Your life belongs to the church leadership
adults.option_d: Your life is controlled by the law
adults.explanation: Colossians 3:3 — "For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God."

adults.question: When Christ who is our life shall appear, what happens according to verse 4?
adults.option_a: Believers shall also appear with him in glory
adults.explanation: Colossians 3:4 — "When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory."

adults.question: What does Paul equate covetousness with in verse 5?
adults.option_a: Idolatry
adults.explanation: Colossians 3:5 — "And covetousness, which is idolatry."

adults.question: What does Paul say about the "old man" and the "new man" in verses 9-10?
adults.option_a: Put off the old man with his deeds and put on the new man renewed in knowledge
adults.option_b: The old man and new man coexist permanently
adults.option_c: The old man was never truly sinful
adults.option_d: The new man is achieved through the law
adults.explanation: Colossians 3:9-10 — "Ye have put off the old man with his deeds; And have put on the new man, which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him."

adults.question: What should rule in believers' hearts according to verse 15?
adults.option_a: The peace of God
adults.explanation: Colossians 3:15 — "And let the peace of God rule in your hearts."

adults.question: How should the word of Christ dwell in believers according to verse 16?
adults.option_a: Richly in all wisdom
adults.explanation: Colossians 3:16 — "Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom."

adults.question: What instruction does Paul give to fathers in verse 21?
adults.option_a: Provoke not your children to anger, lest they be discouraged
adults.option_c: Keep your children away from the church
adults.option_d: Let your children do as they please
adults.explanation: Colossians 3:21 — "Fathers, provoke not your children to anger, lest they be discouraged."

adults.question: What does Paul say servants are truly serving in verse 24?
adults.option_a: The Lord Christ
adults.explanation: Colossians 3:24 — "For ye serve the Lord Christ."

kids.question: What does Paul ask the Colossians to keep doing?
kids.explanation: Colossians 4:2 — "Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving."

kids.question: Who did Paul call a "faithful minister" in this chapter?
kids.explanation: Colossians 4:7 — "All my state shall Tychicus declare unto you, who is a beloved brother, and a faithful minister."

kids.question: Who does Paul say sends greetings — a fellow worker who was a doctor?
kids.explanation: Colossians 4:14 — "Luke, the beloved physician... greet you."

teens.question: How does Paul say believers should walk toward those outside the faith?
teens.option_a: In wisdom, redeeming the time
teens.explanation: Colossians 4:5 — "Walk in wisdom toward them that are without, redeeming the time."

teens.question: How should believers' speech always be according to verse 6?
teens.option_a: With grace, seasoned with salt
teens.explanation: Colossians 4:6 — "Let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt."

teens.question: Who does Paul describe as a fellowprisoner in verse 10?
teens.option_a: Aristarchus
teens.explanation: Colossians 4:10 — "Aristarchus my fellowprisoner saluteth you."

teens.question: What does Paul say about Epaphras in verse 12?
teens.option_a: He is always labouring fervently in prayers for them
teens.option_c: He is building a new church
teens.explanation: Colossians 4:12 — "Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, saluteth you, always labouring fervently for you in prayers."

teens.question: What does Paul instruct regarding this epistle and the one from Laodicea?
teens.option_a: Exchange and read both letters
teens.explanation: Colossians 4:16 — "When this epistle is read among you, cause that it be read also in the church of the Laodiceans; and that ye likewise read the epistle from Laodicea."

adults.question: What does Paul ask the Colossians to pray for regarding his ministry in verse 3?
adults.option_a: That God would open a door of utterance to speak the mystery of Christ
adults.option_b: That he would be released from prison immediately
adults.option_c: That he would receive financial support
adults.option_d: That he would be given political authority
adults.explanation: Colossians 4:3 — "Praying also for us, that God would open unto us a door of utterance, to speak the mystery of Christ."

adults.question: Who is described as "Marcus, sister's son to Barnabas" in verse 10?
adults.option_a: John Mark, about whom the Colossians received commandments
adults.explanation: Colossians 4:10 — "Marcus, sister's son to Barnabas, (touching whom ye received commandments: if he come unto you, receive him.)"

adults.question: Which three men does Paul identify as being "of the circumcision" (Jewish Christians) who were his only fellowworkers?
adults.option_a: Aristarchus, Marcus, and Jesus called Justus
adults.explanation: Colossians 4:10-11 — "Aristarchus... and Marcus... And Jesus, which is called Justus, who are of the circumcision. These only are my fellowworkers."

adults.question: What does Paul say Epaphras has a great zeal for according to verse 13?
adults.option_a: Those in Colossae, Laodicea, and Hierapolis
adults.explanation: Colossians 4:13 — "For I bear him record, that he hath a great zeal for you, and them that are in Laodicea, and them in Hierapolis."

adults.question: What instruction does Paul give to Archippus in verse 17?
adults.option_a: Take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it
adults.option_d: Give the leadership to someone else
adults.explanation: Colossians 4:17 — "Say to Archippus, Take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it."

adults.question: How does Paul sign off the letter in verse 18?
adults.option_a: With his own hand, asking them to remember his bonds
adults.option_b: Through a secretary without personal touch
adults.explanation: Colossians 4:18 — "The salutation by the hand of me Paul. Remember my bonds."

adults.question: Which companion of Paul is mentioned who would later forsake him (cf. 2 Timothy 4:10)?
adults.option_a: Demas
adults.explanation: Colossians 4:14 — "Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas, greet you." In 2 Timothy 4:10, Paul writes that Demas forsook him, "having loved this present world."

adults.question: What is the reference to "the church which is in his house" about in verse 15?
adults.option_a: Nymphas hosted a house church in Laodicea
adults.option_b: Paul had his own church building in Rome
adults.option_c: The Jerusalem temple was called a house
adults.option_d: Peter's home was the first cathedral
adults.explanation: Colossians 4:15 — "Salute... Nymphas, and the church which is in his house."
